name: Neck rolls
instruction: Loosen the neck? Stand tall. Slow, deliberate circles of the head — 5 each way. Breathe through your nose.

name: Shoulder openers
instruction: Open the shoulders? Clasp hands behind your back. Lift chest, drop shoulders back. Hold 20s, release, repeat 3x.

name: Standing forward fold
instruction: Fancy a hang? Find a doorway or quiet corner. Feet hip-width, hinge at the hips, let arms hang. Soft knees. 90 seconds. Let gravity do the work.

name: Wrist stretch
instruction: Wrists feeling tight? Arm out, palm up. Gently pull the fingers down with the other hand. 30s each side, twice.

name: Calf raises
instruction: Wake the calves up? Stand at your desk. Slow rise onto the balls of your feet, slow lower. 3 sets of 15. They've been a footrest for hours.

name: Chest opener
instruction: Open the chest? Find a doorway. Forearm against the frame, step forward. Feel the chest open. 30s each side. Counters everything a keyboard does to your shoulders.

name: Box breathing
instruction: Settle the nervous system? Inhale 4, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4. Repeat for the full break. Regulates the autonomic nervous system in minutes.

name: Physiological sigh
instruction: Downshift in under a minute? Double inhale through the nose, one long exhale through the mouth. Repeat for 3–5 minutes. Fastest reset there is.
Reset the eyes? Look at something 6 metres away for 20 seconds. Then find another. Do this for the whole break.

name: Near-far focus
instruction: Stretch the eye muscles? Hold a finger at arm's length. Focus on it, then on a distant object. Switch slowly, 20 cycles.

name: Plant gaze
instruction: Look at something green? Find the nearest plant or tree out the window. Soften your gaze and let it rest. Green at distance is what your eyes evolved for.

name: Short walk
instruction: Up for a walk? Out the door. Walk for the full duration. Leave the phone on your desk.

name: Stairs
instruction: Find a staircase? Up and down at a pace that gets your heart moving. Stop when the timer does.

name: Stand at the window
instruction: Come to the window? Stand by it. Look outside. Let your eyes travel — near, far, sky. No agenda.

name: Step outside
instruction: Step outside? Even 3 minutes. Real light, real air, weather on your skin. The brain registers this as a different room — that's the point.
Land back in the room? Name 5 things you can see, 4 you can hear, 3 you can feel, 2 you can smell, 1 you can taste. Pulls you out of your head, into the room.

name: Drink water
instruction: Top up? Fill a full glass. Drink it slowly — not in one go. You're probably already mildly dehydrated.

name: Mindful tea or coffee
instruction: Make a slow tea or coffee? Don't multitask. Notice the smell, the warmth of the cup, the first sip. The whole break is the ritual.

name: Next-ride daydream
instruction: Plan the next ride in your head? Where do you start? What's the weather doing? Who's on your wheel? Not on Strava — just in your head.

name: Kit appreciation
instruction: Notice your kit? Look at one piece you love — the bike, a jersey, the shoes. Notice the engineering. Beautiful objects deserve a moment of attention.

name: 8 seconds, 1989
instruction: 1989 Tour de France, final time trial — LeMond starts 50 seconds down on Fignon. He wins the Tour by 8. Closest in history. Sit with that for the break.

name: Pantani's Galibier
instruction: 1998. Stage 15 of the Tour. Cold rain on the Galibier. Pantani attacks 8km from the summit, takes 9 minutes back from Ullrich, wins the Tour. One of the great rides.

name: Voigt's hour, 2014
instruction: September 2014, Grenchen. 43 years old, last race of his career. Jens Voigt rides 51.110 km in a single hour. The record stood for 7 weeks. Sometimes the point is just to try.

name: Eddy Merckx
instruction: Eddy Merckx: 'Don't buy upgrades. Ride up grades.' Sit with it. The break is the recovery interval — you don't need to do anything to earn it.

name: Marco Pantani
instruction: Marco Pantani, asked why he attacked uphill: 'To make the pain shorter.' Stopping is not the opposite of effort. It's part of it.

name: Jens Voigt
instruction: Jens Voigt, when his legs wanted to quit: 'Shut up legs.' But also: 'I get up, I work hard, I go to bed.' Discipline includes the rest.
